steps.label: What They Do
steps.body:
Ateme is a global video compression and delivery vendor. They build the encoders and packagers (TITAN family) that compress live and on-demand video — the studio that prepares your master tape for the world.

Used by tier-one broadcasters, OTT services, and pay-TV operators worldwide.

steps.label: How They Run on Akamai
steps.body:
Ateme TITAN deploys on Linode and LKE. Customers pick:

- Self-managed origin: Ateme on customer-owned Linodes
- Akamai-managed origin: Ateme bundled with Akamai delivery

Compression efficiency + Akamai's flat egress pricing = lower bandwidth and storage cost vs. running on a hyperscaler.

steps.label: When To Position
steps.body: Bring Ateme into deals where the customer needs end-to-end live or VOD encode/package and is leaving an AWS Elemental or hardware-encoder environment. Pairs naturally with EZDRM (DRM) and Yospace (SSAI).
"Ateme = encoder/packager on Akamai. Position when the customer owns the video supply chain end-to-end."

quiz.question: A pay-TV operator needs to migrate their entire live encoding/packaging stack off AWS Elemental. Which QCP do you bring in?

options.explain: Ateme is the QCP for end-to-end video compression and packaging — direct Elemental replacement.
Scaleflex is digital asset management for image/video, not live encoding.

quiz.question: What's the customer's deployment choice with Ateme on Akamai?

options.explain: There's an explicit choice between self- and Akamai-managed.
Customers pick the level of operational ownership they want.
Runs on virtualized Linodes and LKE.
Ateme TITAN is deployed software, not just SaaS.
